machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcP is the XcpP/GspC homolog (inner-membrane platform / secretin gatekeeper). Named only as part of the hxcP-Z homolog set; first gene of the operon, weakest individual quote (no dedicated HxcP characterization paper found). The corpus label 'hxcQ-Z' starts at hxcQ, but the founding/review literature defines the cluster as hxcP-Z; HxcP included as a genuine apparatus component. Lowest-confidence call in this set.

machinery_genes.quote: we investigated HxcQ, the OM component of the second Pseudomonas aeruginosa type II secretion system. We found that HxcQ forms high molecular mass structures resistant to heat and SDS, revealing its secretin nature.
machinery_genes.note: Viarre et al 2009 (JBC) characterize HxcQ as the secretin/outer-membrane channel of the Hxc T2SS; it is a self-piloting lipoprotein (no separate pilotin). Filloux 2011 review also: 'the secretin HxcQ'. GspD family.

machinery_genes.quote: XcpR and HxcR are part of the T2SS and belong to the family of traffic ATPases
machinery_genes.note: HxcR is the GspE/XcpR-family secretion ATPase. The founding paper (Ball et al 2002) characterized an hxcR mutant abolishing LapA secretion; the Filloux 2011 review identifies HxcR as the traffic ATPase. PA0686 in the review table (locus tag for reference only).

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcS is the XcpS/GspF homolog (integral IM core platform). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; role assigned from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism in the apparatus brief (XcpS = GspF).

machinery_genes.quote: Recent work indicates that in the Hxc system HxcT does not form a HPP. This characteristic could be assigned to the lack of conserved residues in HxcT that are otherwise found in XcpT-like proteins
machinery_genes.note: HxcT is the XcpT/GspG-family major pseudopilin; the review explicitly contrasts HxcT with XcpT-like proteins. HPP = hyper-pseudopilus. Durand et al characterized the assembly difference.

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcU = XcpU/GspH homolog (minor pseudopilin). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpU = GspH).

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcV = XcpV/GspI homolog (minor pseudopilin). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpV = GspI).

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcW = XcpW/GspJ homolog (minor pseudopilin). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpW = GspJ).

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcX = XcpX/GspK homolog (minor pseudopilin). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpX = GspK).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the ATPase; XcpY-like)
machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcY = XcpY/GspL homolog (IM platform; the XcpY counterpart binds the ATPase, part of the XcpYRZ complex). Named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpY = GspL).

machinery_genes.quote: The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z.
machinery_genes.note: HxcZ = XcpZ/GspM homolog (IM platform). Terminal gene of the operon; named within the hxcP-Z homolog set; family from the canonical Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpZ = GspM).

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures the pseudopilins; shared with the type IV pilus and the Xcp T2SS)
machinery_genes.quote: The Hxc system also requires the function of the XcpA/PilD protein.
machinery_genes.note: The Hxc system has no dedicated peptidase gene in the hxcP-Z operon; it shares the single XcpA/PilD prepilin peptidase (GspO family) with the Xcp T2SS and the type IV pilus. Encoded outside the hxc cluster but functionally required for Hxc, so listed as apparatus per the brief's GspO/PilD rule.

notes: Hxc is the second, phosphate-starvation-induced T2SS of P. aeruginosa PAO1 ('homologous to xcp'), discovered by Ball, Durand, Lazdunski & Filloux 2002 (Mol Microbiol). It is dedicated to secreting a single substrate, the low-molecular-weight alkaline phosphatase LapA (PA0688, the curated effector for this instance); the related LapC/DING protein is the effector in PA14-type strains (Ball et al 2012). The apparatus is the hxc operon, defined in the literature as hxcP-Z (11 xcp homologs); the corpus label 'hxcQ-Z' undercounts by one (hxcP), which I included as a genuine GspC-family component. All 11 Hxc components (HxcP-HxcZ) plus the shared prepilin peptidase XcpA/PilD (GspO) are listed. Family assignments use the canonical, universally accepted Xcp/Pul/Gsp letter isomorphism (XcpP=GspC, XcpQ=GspD secretin, XcpR=GspE ATPase, XcpS=GspF, XcpT=GspG major pseudopilin, XcpU/V/W/X=GspH/I/J/K minor pseudopilins, XcpY=GspL, XcpZ=GspM, XcpA/PilD=GspO). Genes with dedicated naming quotes: HxcQ (secretin, Viarre 2009 JBC + Filloux 2011 review), HxcR (ATPase, Filloux 2011), HxcT (major pseudopilin, Filloux 2011), XcpA/PilD (shared peptidase, Filloux 2011). The remaining seven (HxcP, S, U, V, W, X, Y, Z) are covered by the verbatim collective quote 'The hxc cluster contains the 11 xcp homologs, hxcP-Z' (Filloux 2011); the founding Ball 2002 paper (Mol Microbiol, paywalled, no PMC) is the primary characterization but its gene-by-gene table could not be quoted directly, so the open-access Filloux 2011 review is used for the verbatim naming. HxcP is the lowest-confidence individual call (operon-edge, no dedicated characterization). Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the substrates LapA/LapB/LapC (effectors), the PhoB/PhoR phosphate-starvation regulators, and the Sec/Tat inner-membrane translocon. Locus tags appearing in source tables (HxcQ=PA0685, HxcR=PA0686, LapA=PA0688) are recorded in notes only, never as the gene field, per the locus-tag rule.
